title: Salty Flaky Korean Beef Dumplings
description: A savory and spicy Korean dish featuring ras el hanout and fermented ingredients, perfect for a midnight craving. This halal-friendly recipe combines the richness of beef with the depth of Korean chili flakes and the warmth of ras el hanout.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, ras el hanout, Korean chili flakes, fermented Korean chili paste, soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger. Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper.
Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
When ready to assemble the dumplings, lay a dumpling wrapper on a clean surface. Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of the wrapper.

Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edge of the wrapper. Fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point. Press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
In a large pot, bring 4-6 quarts of water to a boil. Reduce the heat to a simmer.
Carefully add the dumplings to the pot, being careful not to overcrowd. Cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the dumplings float to the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the pot with a slotted spoon and serve hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
